approach: Dispatch the review prompt to pane %42 via `talk send`, poll `talk ping` in a backgrounded until-loop so the foreground stays free, then capture the worker's final output with `talk read` once idle.

commands.cmd: talk send %42 "Review /home/fenrir/code/coinsasia/backend/backend-api-golang/internal/exchangeapi/auth/handlers.go and list the top 3 security risks. 3 short bullets is enough."
commands.purpose: Deliver the review task to the worker pane atomically (single paste + Enter) so it starts immediately.

commands.cmd: until talk ping %42 >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 3; done
commands.purpose: Wait for the worker's Claude Code title to flip from Braille-spinner (busy) back to the idle glyph, without blocking the foreground. Run in background so the notification fires the moment the loop exits (~60s).
Capture the tail of the worker's scrollback once idle. 200 lines comfortably covers the ~60-line noisy progress stream plus the final 3-bullet summary so the driver can surface the risks to the user.

rationale: The old skill's canonical dispatch pattern is send -> ping-poll -> read. `talk tail` is explicitly forbidden (blocking TUI); a bare `talk read` before idle would race the still-streaming spinner output; asking the worker to write a handoff file would add a round-trip the scenario doesn't need since the final summary fits easily in a 200-line read window. Backgrounding the poll loop keeps the driver responsive and leverages the completion notification instead of sleep-then-check.
